indicators.label: RBI Repo Rate
indicators.source: Reserve Bank of India — MPC Statement June 2026
indicators.interpretation: MPC voted unanimously to hold repo rate at 5.25% for the third consecutive meeting, maintaining a neutral stance. The previous rate was 5.50% before cumulative 125 bps of cuts since Feb 2025. With Brent crude now at ~$83/bbl (vs RBI's war-era projection at elevated levels), the path has opened for a possible cut at the August 2026 MPC if the crude-led inflation relief sustains.

indicators.label: CPI Inflation YoY (May 2026)
indicators.source: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) — CPI Press Release May 2026
indicators.interpretation: CPI at 3.93% in May 2026 remains below the RBI's 4% midpoint target. Food inflation (CFPI) climbed to 4.78%. The sequential rise from 2.75% in January to 3.93% in May signals gradual inflationary pressure, but the sharp crude-oil crash post US-Iran peace deal (June 15) should help restrain the June print. The RBI had revised its FY27 CPI forecast to 5.1% during the June MPC; crude relief could result in a downward revision at the August meeting.

indicators.label: WPI Inflation YoY (May 2026)
indicators.source: Ministry of Commerce & Industry — WPI Press Release May 2026
indicators.interpretation: WPI surged to 9.68% in May under the revised 2022-23 base series, driven by Fuel & Power inflation of 30.33% YoY, mineral oils +49.82%, crude petroleum +61.51%. Manufactured products (63% weight) rose 7.48%. This prints from when crude was over $100/bbl; the June print (due mid-July) should moderate substantially given Brent has fallen from ~$124/bbl at war peak to ~$83/bbl now.

indicators.label: GDP Growth FY26 (Provisional Actual)
indicators.source: MoSPI — Provisional Estimates of GDP for FY2025-26
indicators.interpretation: India's FY26 real GDP grew 7.7%, the fastest pace in three years, beating consensus of 7.2-7.5%. Q4 FY26 clocked 7.8%. Manufacturing expanded 10.7%; financial services and real estate grew 10.4%; trade, hotels, transport grew 11%. Strong GFCF (+10.8% in Q4) signals investment-led momentum. India Inc Q4 FY26 aggregate net profit grew 25.3% YoY — the highest in nine quarters.

indicators.label: GDP Growth FY27 RBI Forecast
indicators.source: Reserve Bank of India — MPC Statement June 2026
indicators.interpretation: RBI revised FY27 GDP growth forecast down from 6.9% to 6.6% at the June 2026 MPC, citing global uncertainties, geopolitical tensions, higher energy costs, and monsoon risks. With crude now lower and a US-Iran peace deal reducing supply shocks, the actual FY27 GDP may come in closer to 6.8-7.0% if rate cuts materialize at August MPC.

indicators.label: IIP Industrial Production Growth (April 2026)
indicators.source: MoSPI — Index of Industrial Production April 2026 (new base year 2022-23)
indicators.interpretation: Industrial output grew 4.9% YoY in April 2026 under the new IIP series (base year 2022-23). Manufacturing rose 6.2%. Capital goods surged 16% YoY — strongest segment — reflecting public capex and infrastructure spending. Consumer durables grew only 4.3% and consumer non-durables 2.8%, signalling uneven demand recovery with investment ahead of consumption.

indicators.label: FII Net Flows (June 2026 MTD)
indicators.source: Moneycontrol FII/DII Activity — NSE+BSE+MSEI combined provisional
indicators.interpretation: FIIs have been net sellers in June MTD at -₹46,230 Cr as of June 15. However, the trend is improving: FIIs turned net buyers on June 15 itself (+₹200 Cr) following the US-Iran peace deal. With crude lower, the rupee firming, and the India-US BTA nearing finalisation (talks June 23-24), conditions are building for a reversal of FII selling — though a sustained turn has not yet materialised.

indicators.label: DII Net Flows (June 2026 MTD)
indicators.source: Moneycontrol FII/DII Activity — NSE+BSE+MSEI combined provisional
indicators.interpretation: DIIs net bought ₹61,137 Cr in June MTD through June 15, substantially more than offsetting FII selling of ₹46,230 Cr. DII YTD purchases in 2026 have crossed ₹4.16 lakh crore (vs FII outflows of ~₹2.7 lakh crore). Monthly SIP inflows stood at ₹31,115 Cr in April 2026 (+18% YoY). Structural domestic investor participation is providing a resilient floor beneath Indian equities.

indicators.label: USD/INR Exchange Rate
indicators.source: Twelve Data / BookMyForex — USD/INR historical June 15 2026 close
indicators.interpretation: The rupee closed at 94.71 per dollar on June 15 — its strongest in five weeks — after the US-Iran peace deal drove crude lower and reduced India's import bill pressure. The rupee had weakened to ~96.8 in May at peak conflict. On June 16 pre-market, the rate is effectively unchanged at ~94.71 as the market opens. A firmer rupee is mildly negative for IT/pharma exporters but positive for macro stability and inflation.

indicators.label: Brent Crude Oil (USD/bbl)
indicators.source: MarketScreener / Straits Times — Brent crude June 16 2026 early session
indicators.interpretation: Brent is trading at ~$83.24/bbl on June 16 morning — rebounding slightly (+0.3%) from the June 15 close of $83.34 as markets assess concerns about the pace of supply restoration through the Strait of Hormuz. Crude had spiked above $124/bbl during the West Asia conflict peak. At $83/bbl, India's import bill, CAD pressure, and inflation trajectory are all meaningfully improved vs the war-period scenario.

indicators.label: India 10Y G-Sec Yield
indicators.source: CountryEconomy.com / LiveMint — India 10-year bond yield June 15 2026
indicators.interpretation: India's 10-year G-sec yield fell to 6.83% on June 15 — a 12-week low — as the US-Iran peace deal drove oil lower and reduced inflation expectations. Falling bond yields reduce borrowing costs across the economy, support bank NIMs on existing books, and re-rate rate-sensitive sectors. The crude relief provides a pathway for a potential 25 bps rate cut at the August MPC if inflation data cooperates.

indicators.label: Fiscal Deficit FY26 (Provisional Actuals)
indicators.source: Moneycontrol — India FY26 fiscal deficit at 4.4%, in line with govt estimates
indicators.interpretation: India's FY26 fiscal deficit came in at 4.4% of GDP (₹15.19 lakh crore), meeting the government's revised target. For FY27, the April deficit has already touched 21.4% of the annual target as revenue receipts fell sharply — this is seasonal and expected. The government's FY27 fiscal target remains 4.4% of GDP per Union Budget. Fiscal discipline is positive for sovereign rating trajectory and bond market confidence.

indicators.label: GST Collections (May 2026)
indicators.source: Economic Times — GST collections rise 3.2% to ₹1.94 lakh crore in May
indicators.interpretation: Gross GST in May 2026 was ₹1.94 lakh crore, up 3.2% YoY. The moderation from April's record ₹2.42 lakh crore is seasonal. Import-linked GST surged 19.7% (driven by oil prices) while domestic revenue fell 2.3% YoY — suggesting underlying domestic consumption softness. April-May combined GST rose 6.2% to ₹4.37 lakh crore, broadly in line with nominal GDP growth.

indicators.label: Southwest Monsoon 2026 — Progress & IMD Forecast
indicators.value: 28% below normal rainfall as of June 15; season forecast 90% of LPA
indicators.source: IMD / PIB — LRF Second Stage + IMD Daily Weather Briefing June 15 2026
indicators.interpretation: India's cumulative monsoon rainfall as of June 15 is running 28% below the Long Period Average — a meaningful early-season deficit. IMD's second-stage long-range forecast pegs the 2026 Southwest Monsoon at 90% of LPA (below normal) with 60% probability of deficiency — worst since 2015. Monsoon progress has stalled at the Western Ghats; Central India, Northwest India, and Peninsular India all face below-normal rain through June. The monsoon break (June 16-21) is expected to further delay advancement into Central and Northwest India. A deficient monsoon risks higher food inflation, stressed agri-NBFC portfolios, and weaker rural FMCG demand.

marketState.headline: Transition to Bullish — US-Iran peace deal has resolved the dominant risk factor; crude crash, softer yields, and FII green shoots (net buyers on June 15) tilt the balance; monsoon deficit and sticky WPI remain the structural drag.

boosting.detail: US-Iran peace deal (announced June 15, formal signing Switzerland June 19) ends the Strait of Hormuz blockade risk. Brent at ~$83/bbl vs $124/bbl war peak — the single largest macro risk for India is now in structural retreat.

DIIs net bought ₹61,137 Cr in June MTD as of June 15. YTD DII buying crosses ₹4.16 lakh crore. SIP flows structurally firm at ₹31,115 Cr/month. Domestic institutions are now the primary price-setter in Indian equities.

FIIs turned net buyers (+₹200 Cr) on June 15 — first net positive session in weeks — after the peace deal. If sustained, this would reverse months of outflows and unlock significant re-rating potential.

boosting.factor: Falling crude + softer yields + stronger INR
boosting.detail: Brent ~$83/bbl, India 10Y at 6.83% (12-week low), INR at 94.71. All three key transmission channels (inflation, borrowing cost, currency stability) are moving in the right direction simultaneously.
USTR Greer visits June 22, talks June 23-24. Commerce Secretary says only 'final touches' remain. Signing expected before mid-July. Removes Section 301 tariff overhang for IT, pharma, textiles.
FY26 GDP 7.7% (3-year high), fiscal deficit 4.4% of GDP (met target), India Inc Q4 net profit +25.3% YoY. Corporate balance sheets entering FY27 from a position of strength.

lagging.detail: Cumulative rainfall 28% below LPA as of June 15. Monsoon stalled at Western Ghats; 16 states waiting for rain. IMD LRF: 90% of LPA season-total, 60% probability of deficiency. Structural risk to food inflation, rural demand (FMCG, two-wheelers, agri-NBFCs).

May WPI of 9.68% driven by war-era crude prices. Likely to moderate sharply in the June print (due mid-July) given crude crash. But sticky manufactured goods inflation (7.48%) keeps input cost pressure real for industrials.
Despite June 15's positive single-day, FIIs remain net sellers -₹46,230 Cr for June MTD. A genuine multi-day reversal needs to be confirmed before calling the trend changed.

lagging.factor: RBI on hold — August cut not guaranteed
lagging.detail: Repo rate at 5.25% with neutral stance. A cut requires June CPI to remain below 4% AND monsoon outlook to improve. If monsoon deficit deepens, the RBI may stay on hold even into October.

lagging.factor: Tata Motors/JLR FY26 near-zero FCF drag
lagging.detail: JLR flagged 'close to zero' FY26 free cash flow and macro risks on June 16, sending Tata Motors down 5%. A reminder that individual stock-specific risks remain despite the improving macro backdrop.

marketState.narration: The market is in an accelerating transition toward a cleaner bullish posture. The removal of the geopolitical risk premium — the US-Iran peace deal collapsing crude from $124/bbl to $83/bbl — is the most significant single macro event since the West Asia conflict began. India is its direct beneficiary: lower import bill, lower CAD, lower inflation, lower bond yields, and a firmer rupee. FIIs turned net buyers on June 15 for the first time in weeks. The Nifty briefly touched 24,000 intraday on June 15 before closing at 23,854. On June 16, GIFT Nifty signals a muted but positive open (+4.5 pts). Key resistance is 24,000-24,200; support at 23,700-23,800. The market is positioned to make a decisive upside break IF the June CPI print (due late June) confirms crude relief is flowing through to retail prices AND if FII buying sustains. Until then, the below-normal monsoon, hot WPI (though set to cool), and the IT sector's structural AI headwinds cap the upside momentum. Net read: upgrade to Bullish tilt with Medium-High confidence — lean into rate-sensitives (NBFC, realty), domestic capex (Capital Goods, Defence), and quality private banks on dips. Stay cautious on export-facing IT until BTA is signed and on rural FMCG until monsoon deficit signals resolve.
marketState.method: Research interpretation of the sourced macro indicators + institutional flows + index trend. Recomputed at each scheduled refresh. Not investment advice.
